figures.caption: Figure 2: The ω-VPA recognizing Step and ProperStep. A transition (q,X, a, q′, γ) is depicted by an edge from q to q′ labeled by a,X | γ. Here, c, r, and s stand for arbitrary letters from Σc, Σr, and Σs, respectively.

Figure 3: Turning an PDA into an ω-PDA while preserving universality and normalization. A transition (q,X, a, q′, γ) is depicted by an edge from q to q′ labeled by a,X | γ. Here, ∗ represents an arbitrary letter from Σ (but not #!) and † represents an arbitrary letter from Σ ∪ {#}. Furthermore, the thick transition connects every state of A to q′s1 .
